  • Maximize Your ROI: You've already invested in SAP. By upgrading, you're maximizing that investment. You're unlocking the full potential of your system and getting the most value out of your license.
  • Practice makes Perfect: It's like training a muscle. The more often you upgrade, the easier the process becomes. Regular upgrades equip your team with the know-how for faster, smoother projects with fewer disruptions. Customers like Hitachi High-Tech have reduced their upgrade projects to just a single month by adopting a regular upgrade approach. Read Hitachi´s complete upgrade story.

Build your Upgrade Routine:

Establishing your governance strategy and plan to fit your individual operation and needs is key. SAP recommends that you run on the latest release or at least one below the latest, aligning your IT change management and business release strategy, as well as your project schedule, with SAP S/4HANA release timelines. As announced with the new SAP S/4HANA release and maintenance strategy, new releases are delivered every second year, with three feature and support packages in between, offering you greater flexibility and easier-to-consume innovation.

SAP is Here to Help You Every Step of the Way

Upgrading doesn't have to be daunting. SAP provides a wealth of resources to ensure a smooth transition:

  • SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade, a methodology for implementing SAP solutions, guides your team through the upgrade by prompting deliverables and tasks at the right time.  
  • SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades helps assess your upgrade efforts with analysis of your custom code, compatibility scope usage, and add-on usage.  
  • SAP guidance and resources for technical upgrade execution are included with software subscription., including tasks like operating system upgrade, database upgrade, and the technical installation of SAP S/4HANA upgrades. Please remember that as an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition customer, you're responsible for planning upgrades, requesting services, and handling post-upgrade tasks.
  • The Enterprise Support Academy and dedicated SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Value Maps* help upskill your team.  
  • SAP Services and Support including SAP Enterprise Support available with all cloud offerings, and several premium plans, can bring you a dedicated expert assistant for the short or long term.
  • SAP Partner Finder can help you identify a partner from our vast ecosystem for implementation services.  

 

In conclusion: By embracing regular upgrades and a clean core approach, you unlock a multitude of benefits, including accelerated innovation adoption and increased security and compliance. Building your upgrade routine and leveraging valuable tools, services and support from SAP and its ecosystem will result in a smoother upgrade journey.

So don’t let your system reach a point where a costly and time-consuming overhaul becomes essential to avoid slowdown. Maximize the value of your SAP investment - embrace agility and upgrade to stay ahead of the competition.

Read the whitepaper, "Embrace agility and upgrade for success."

Join our Upgrade dedicated SAP Community

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team

entry.title: Be Part of Tomorrow: Join SAP's Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1, 2025!
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce the launch of the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025, featuring 50 exciting new projects aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration with our valued SAP customers and partners.

In today’s fast-paced business world, staying ahead is more important than ever. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative presents a unique opportunity to gain early insights into SAP’s product developments. By participating, you’ll work directly with our developers and product managers, helping shape the future of our products.

Register until March 28, 2025! Don’t miss out on the opportunity to have your voice heard and influence the next generation of SAP solutions.

To help you explore the available projects, we’ve compiled a detailed list for your convenience. Check out the full list here or follow the links below to discover the exciting opportunities:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

SAP Customer Experience

Data & Analytics

  • Redesigning the Dashboard Experience
  • SAP HANA Cloud Elastic Compute Node
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations

  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud

SAP Globalization

  • Planned enhancements to Run statutory reports to handle Draft Report reconciliation

Lifecycle Management

SAP Integration Experience Feedback goes SAP Customer Engagement Initiative

  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for External Workforce
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Hire to Retire Cloud
  • Assisted Assessment of Aligned Business APIs for Source-to-Pay

Others

  • Research into AI-Assisted User Learning Rollout for Adoption
  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX
  • Shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis
  • The AI-Driven Future of New Product Introduction (NPI)

We believe that collaboration is key to sparking innovation. By working alongside our customers and partners, we can jointly shape the future of SAP’s products and transform business operations.

Let’s take this journey together. Together, we have the power to drive progress and transform industries.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Import Financial Plan Data - Understanding the Plan Data Scope
entry.content:
The Import Financial Plan Data app is available in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private edition. It allows key users to import plan data line items from CSV files.

Understanding Plan Data Scope

This blog post will explain how the plan data scope works and how it helps you avoid accidently overwriting data.
Please keep in mind that the Import Financial Plan Data app is only an interface for uploading data into the system. The data has to be maintained and reviewed manually before uploading it, as  only technical checks are performed (for example, checking whether master data exists in the target system) and no business logic is integrated.

Let us explore how to use the Import Financial Plan Data app in a simple scenario for cost center planning.

Example Scenarios

Example 1: Initial Import
In the example provided, you intend to import four plan data records. Since this is the first time the application is being used, no records are present in the database. This means four new records will be incorporated from the CSV file, as shown in the following screens:

After importing the plan data, you then see a success message that allows you to run the Display Financial Plan Data report directly. This report displays the plan data on the corresponding accounts, indicating that the outcome is as expected.

Example 2: Correcting Data
Suppose you notice that one of the amounts in your file was incorrect. To correct this, simply change the amount from 100 to 150 USD, and import the file again. The import process informs you that the existing plan data will be adapted within the plan data scope and that four records will be incorporated from the CSV file.

After the successful import, the Display Financial Plan Data report shows the corrected amount of 150 USD.

Example 3: Adding Missing Accounts
In another example, you may have forgotten to upload the plan data for a specific account. In this case, you merely need to reimport the file, including the new account.

The system notifies you that the existing plan data will be adapted within the plan data scope and that five records will be incorporated from the CSV file.
Consequently, the Display Financial Plan Data report now displays the plan data for the missing account as well.

 

Example 4: Handling Complex Scenarios
Let us now examine how the system handles more complex scenarios or repetitive imports.
To reverse specific records before importing a new CSV file, the system creates a logical query based on the dimensions included in the file. Dimensions marked with an X in row #3 are concatenated with AND, while the different members in this column are concatenated with OR. For dimensions without an X, the members are not specified, so records for all members of that dimension are reversed.

Note:
This may reverse data values for which there is no corresponding row in the source file because the selection defined by the plan data scope reverses all values for which the following is true, for example:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND (Cost Center = "0017101101" OR Cost Center = "17101201")

In example 3, you mistakenly planned on cost center 1710201, which should not have been planned on. The data you imported in this file is incorrect, and now you wish to send only three records instead of five.
To correct this, you reimport the file with these three records, but you leave the X for the cost center dimension in the file.

This action results in the reversal of the records meeting the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND Cost Center = "0017101101"

The erroneous cost center data remains unchanged, so the incorrect data for cost center 1710201 is still present in the system after this import.

To correct the erroneous data, you need to remove the X from the cost center column.

This action results in the reversal of the records meeting the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025"
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"

This sets the records for cost centers not specified in the CSV file to zero.

 

Example 5: Updating and Adding Records
In this last example, you want to correct one existing record for 2025 from 150 USD to 200 USD and add new records for 2026.

Let’s assume that the data in your system looks as follows:

By importing the CSV file as shown above, the system reverses the plan records for both cost centers and both years. Note, however, that even records that were not part of the uploaded CSV file are also reversed because of the following criteria:
Plan Category = "PLN"
AND (General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2025" OR General Ledger Fiscal Year = "2026")
AND Posting Period = "011"
AND Company Code = "1710"
AND (Cost Center = "0017101101" OR Cost Center ="0017101201")

To resolve this, you need to split your data into two upload files.

One CSV file contains the record that needs to be corrected and where the X is set for all dimensions, including the G/L account.

This changes the amount from 150 USD to 200 USD, while all other records remain unchanged.

The second file contains the new records for 2026, where you don’t need to set the X for the G/L account dimension.

After both CSV files have been uploaded, the data in your system looks as follows:

Conclusion:
To prevent data from being overwritten unintentionally, uploaded data must be disjointed. A clear understanding of the plan data scope ensures that records can be added, updated, and reversed safely.

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 03
entry.content:
To complete the blog series about innovations in SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) coming in SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Initial Stack I Feature Pack Stack 01 I Feature Pack Stack 02), I'd now like to share delta information about the key MDG features coming with Feature Pack Stack 03.

Enabling field extensibility in customer and supplier views (cloud-ready mode)

To increase the effectiveness in master data governance of business-partner data in cloud-ready mode, we have enabled the following field extensibility capabilities: 

  • Apply field extensibility (custom fields) not only in the business-partner core view (BUT000), but also in customer and supplier views.
  • Extend fields in the following views:
    • Customer core view (KNA1)
    • Customer company code view (KNB1)
    • Customer sales area core view (KNVV)
    • Supplier core view (LFA1)
    • Supplier company code view (LFB1)
    • Supplier purchasing org view (LFM1)

Field extensibility in supplier view (cloud-ready mode)

Assignment of data controllers to a business partner in the classic mode of central governance

It is now possible to manually assign one or more data controllers to a business partner and set the "Invalid Assignment Status" for existing data controllers. Additionally, the system can immediately derive data controllers when the corresponding organizational units are maintained.

Setting the "Invalid Assignment Status" for existing data controllers

Enhanced validation of changes in financial data 

To avoid errors in the governance of financial data, checks for the most business-critical attributes can be made with remote function calls to target systems to make sure that proposed changes in MDG are validated before change request activation.

Defining target systems for remote validation and display of validation results in MDG processing

Gen AI-based assisted change for custom objects and summary of mass changes*

To further increase the effectiveness through AI in central governance​, we have enabled AI-assisted changes for reuse entities in custom objects and the AI-based summary of change requests with multiple objects (mass changes).

AI-assisted change and AI-based summary of changes

(* Please note that these Gen AI-based features are only available with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition)

See also the presentation outlining the MDG delta content coming with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03.

To get the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS03 – What’s New
entry.content:
We have recently released the latest updates for SAP EWM with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 3 update. The enhancements of this release further improve our Advanced Shipping & Receiving integration and functionalities, complementing the features we delivered in the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 2 release. Furthermore, with this release we introduced a new Machine Learning based feature, called “Predictive Labor Demand Planning”, empowering shift managers at the warehouse to more accurately and efficiently plan out the tasks for the day. And to start today’s deep dive, we will begin with it.

PREDICITVE LABOR DEMAND PLANNING

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release, we deliver the new Machine-Learning based predictive labor demand planning (LDP) that can be used to predict planned workload data based on the picking and packing outbound processes. This enables warehouse shift managers and supervisors to plan resources and workloads more efficiently. Very summarily, LDP uses historical workload records to calculate a planned duration for warehouse workload records, and then use those records to plan outbound delivery orders.

 Historical workload records contain information about executed warehouse tasks (WTs) in the warehouse. They are not stored in an archive as all data is saved in the database, and outdated records are deleted. These records are only written for picking and packing tasks when a warehouse order is confirmed. These records contain application information that is mainly based on warehouse task data, with additional data from the assigned outbound delivery order.

The planned duration is a key component of a planned workload record. In labor management, you can use engineered labor standards to calculate the planned duration, but in labor demand planning, this is not necessary. Instead, labor demand planning uses historical workload records to calculate the planned duration for planned workload records.

Please note that if you have already set up engineered labor standards, they will still be used to calculate the planned duration. In this case, the system will not calculate based on historical workload records. You can also use engineered labor standards for one area of your warehouse and use calculations based on historical workload records for another area.

Outbound delivery order (ODO) processing includes picking and packing steps before the ODO item can be staged and loaded. You can use labor demand planning to get better visibility into the planned workload that is expected from outbound delivery orders. This allows you to predict peaks in the workload and balance the workload of the warehouse accordingly. The way LDP calculates ODO planned workloads is the following:

  1. The system searches for historical data that belongs to similar ODO items. The resulting data forms the basis of the calculations for the required data of the current ODO item.
  2. The system identifies the picking location, preferably by using rough bin determination.
  3. The system determines the external process step that is most used by similar ODO items.
  4. If similar ODO items require packing steps, the system calculates the packing steps based on the packing-picking ratio of the similar ODO items. The packing-picking ratio indicates how often the pick step of an outbound delivery order with similar attributes was followed by a pack step and is calculated from the historical workload data. Single pieces may need a pack step while whole pallet picks don’t need to be packed in addition.

 With LDP, it is possible to view planned workload durations directly in the warehouse monitor, allowing for better visibility and control over task planning. This functionality improves operational efficiency by streamlining the planning process and ensuring more accurate and timely execution of warehouse tasks as well as reduce inefficiencies by predicting the time required for tasks such as picking, packing, and outbound deliveries.

This is only a first step for Predictive Labor Demand Planning, and we plan to build on top of this feature with future releases. But we will reveal those steps at a future date.

ADVANCED SHIPPING & RECEIVING UPDATES

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release we continue to further extend the range of functionalities available with Advanced Shipping & Receiving as well as expand on existing ones. In the interest of time and convenience, I want to briefly touch upon every ASR update in one or two paragraphs, and we begin with integration to Global Trade Service:

  • Integration of Decentralized EWM and TM with GTS – This is a new integration of decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) and Transportation Management (TM) with SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) which supports the execution of the outbound, or export, process. It enables users to conduct compliance checks for freight units and freight orders and complete export declaration for consignments and freight orders.

  • Search for Advanced Shipping and Receiving Deliveries in Waves – In this release, we further build upon a feature we delivered in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, by enabling users to search for waves, wave items, and wave-relevant warehouse requests that are part of Advanced Shipping and Receiving deliveries in the Maintain Waves app (transaction /SCWM/WAVE) and the warehouse monitor (transaction /SCWM/MON).
  • Custom Checks for Loading Materials onto a Freight Order – We also provide BAdIs that add customer-specific checks for handling units or warehouse requests before loading execution in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. The checks take place during outbound delivery, and allow for the customer’s own logic for loading checks in:
    • The radio frequency (RF) screen before loading, where the system can show special warning or error message to the RF user
    • The App "Load or Unload Freight Orders", where the same or a similar BAdI is called when the loading execution function is used
    • The enterprise warehouse management (EWM) desktop transaction /SCWM/LOAD
  • TM Updates for Unpacked Products with Actual EWM Weight and Volume – With this update, when information regarding weight and volume for unpacked products in an outbound delivery order item is updated in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the relevant documents in Transportation Management (TM) are updated with the adjusted EWM weight and volume information. These updates are made after the “Ready for Shipping” status is set in EWM. The updated weight and volume are sent on product item level and on handling unit header level.

CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENTS

To round of today’s overview, I would like to also go over some of the final topics that came to be as a result of the most recent Customer Connect program:

  • Add Short Text to Goods Issue and Cancellation - With this feature, it is now possible to provide a short text for goods issues and cancellations in several apps. A text of up to 50 characters can now be manually entered, for example, to add an explanation or other information to the goods movement in the following apps: “Manage Physical Stock – Products” (F5340); “Manage Warehouse Documents” (F5341). This text can be seen in the relevant material documents in other components of SAP S/4HANA using the “Display Material Document List” app. If synchronous goods movements are used from Inventory Management to Extended Warehouse Management (only available in EWM embedded in SAP S/4HANA), text can be entered in the “Post Goods Movement” app (transaction MIGO). The text is visible in the corresponding goods movement document in EWM, and can be seen in the monitor node at Documents -> Warehouse Task. This functionality has also been added to the consume and scrap actions in the “Warehouse Physical Stock by Product” API.

  • Scanning Alternative HU IDs During RF Processing - With this feature, it is now possible to scan alternative handling unit (HU) IDs on vendor labels which the system converts them to internal Extended Warehouse Management Handling Unit IDs. Converting the alternative HU IDs in RF transactions removes the need to re-label the HUs or create custom RF screens. Note that if multiple HU IDs are assigned to one alternative HU ID, the relevant internal HU ID can be selected.

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above mentioned topics as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release, I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP EWM in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 – What’s New” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: SAP Field Service Management 2502: Advanced AI, Planning Simulations, and PM Order Integration
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management 2502 builds on our strategic investment in AI for field service organizations, introducing advanced planning simulations, expanded multilingual AI support, and the ability to query jobs based on user-defined fields (UDFs) and custom label translations. This release also delivers a native integration with SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) orders using SAP Integration Suite for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, further strengthening customers' integration landscapes. Additionally, 2502 brings several usability and workflow enhancements, simplifying daily operations while boosting efficiency for planners, dispatchers, and field technicians.

In this blog, we’ll explore the most notable SAP FSM 2502 features, along with demo videos showcasing key highlights.

👉 For the complete list of all features in the 2502 release, click here.

Key Highlights in SAP Field Service Management 2502:

  • Planning Simulations – Review proposed scheduling changes and compare different scheduling policies.
  • Generative AI – Multilingual support (all languages except Tamil) and natural language filtering using UDFs and custom label translations.
  • AI-Based Scheduling – Gain deeper insights into auto-scheduling decisions.
  • Integration – Standard integration with SAP PM orders for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, plus service contract integration.
  • Smartforms Enhancements – Add Smartforms to Service Calls, filter by Smartforms, and edit forms from the Partner Portal.
  • Service Map Enhancements – Support for up to 250 activities/day* and localized units (kilometers/miles).
  • Expanded Language Support – Now supporting Catalan, Croatian, Greek, Kazakh, Serbian (Latin), Slovenian, and Ukrainian.

Note: The Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering features require a customer to be licensed for SAP AI Units. For more information about licensing, please contact your SAP Account Executive.

*Requires HERE Maps routing service.


Artificial Intelligence


Planning Simulations

SAP Field Service Management 2502 introduces Planning Simulations, empowering schedulers to review proposed scheduling changes and compare different scheduling policies before finalizing assignments. This enhancement provides greater control and flexibility, ensuring optimized workforce utilization. Learn more about this feature here.

Planning Simulations

Natural Language Filtering – Additional Language, UDF, & Translation Support

A major enhancement in this release is Natural Language Filtering, which now supports custom label translations and UDF-based searches. This allows organizations to search using company-specific terminology and in their preferred language, enabling a more localized and intuitive AI experience. Find out more details here.

Translation and UDF support for Natural Language Filtering

Generative AI capabilities are now available in all supported languages (except Tamil), making AI-driven insights more accessible to global users. See the full details here.

Expanded multi-lingual support for Generative AI features

AI Impact

According to SAP customer benchmarking, field service organizations leveraging these AI-powered features achieve:

⬆️ 12.5% increase in dispatcher productivity
⬆️5% improvement in first-time fix rates
⬇️ 15% reduction in time spent reviewing and planning service requests

Deeper Insights into Auto-Scheduling Decisions

Reports for AI-based scheduling now offer greater transparency and control, giving planners visibility into technician assignments, travel times, and re-optimization iterations. Detailed scheduling reports provide insights into how decisions are made, improving transparency and increasing autonomy to fine-tune scheduling policies with confidence. Explore this enhancement further here.

Deeper Insights into Auto-Scheduling Decisions


Integration


Integration with Plant Maintenance Orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition

New out-of-the-box integration using SAP Integration Suite connects maintenance planning with scheduling and dispatch, ensuring real-time synchronization between SAP S/4HANA PM orders and SAP FSM. This simplifies integration by reducing custom development efforts and ensuring scalability and consistency across IT landscapes. Read the full details here.

Plant Maintenance order integration with SAP FSM

 Integration flow

Integration of Service Contracts

Service contract synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and SAP FSM ensures real-time access to contract data, SLAs, and response times. This reduces integration complexity, improves performance, and enhances service execution by ensuring field service teams have the right contract details at the right time. Check out the documentation here.

User Experience & Usability Enhancements


Expanded Language Support

SAP FSM now supports Catalan, Croatian, Greek, Kazakh, Serbian (Latin script), Slovenian, and Ukrainian, enabling more users to work in their preferred language. Click here to see all supported languages.

Expanded Language Support - Catalan, Croatian, Greek, Kazakh, Serbian (Latin script), Slovenian, and Ukrainian

 

Master Data Management

Equipment: Improved UX and Measurement Points

With expanded on-screen insights, Equipment Master Data now provides richer details on service history, measurement points, and overdue services, giving users a complete historical view to support well-informed planning. See more here. 

Equipment Master Data Updates

Smartforms

Add Smartforms to Service Calls & Filter by Smartforms

Previously, adding Smartforms to Service Calls or searching for them was not possible, making it difficult to locate specific forms in large lists. Now, Planners and Schedulers can add Smartforms to Service Calls, search for them, and view their status, reducing time spent managing large volumes of forms. For additional insights, click here.

Add Smartforms to Service Calls & Filter by Smartforms

Manage Smartforms in the Partner Portal

Partner Managers can now view, edit, and save Smartforms directly within the Partner Portal, providing greater flexibility when managing technician activities. This enhancement allows Smartforms to be completed on behalf of technicians, improving workflow efficiency and ensuring accurate data validation before closing activities. See the full details here. 

Manage Smartforms in the Partner Portal

Service Map

Enhanced Routing with HERE Maps

Dispatchers can now visualize routes for up to 250 activities per day using the HERE Maps routing service, overcoming previous Google Maps limitations. This enhancement improves route planning and visibility, especially when managing high-volume job assignments. More info here.

Enhanced Routing with HERE Maps

Service Map Localization for Distance Units

The Service Map now automatically adjusts distance units based on the user's standard regional preferences, displaying miles or kilometers depending on the region. This enhancement improves usability and localization, ensuring dispatchers and technicians see distances in their preferred unit of measurement. More details here.

Service Map Localization for Distance Units

Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2502 enhances the entire field service value chain with AI-driven planning simulations, standard SAP S/4HANA PM integration, and usability improvements that simplify daily operations.

We invite you to explore these new capabilities, share your feedback, and help shape the future of SAP FSM by submitting your influence requests here. Stay tuned—even more innovations are on the way in 2505!

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. It would also be great to hear directly from you which innovations you think could be the most impactful for future releases. For related posts and more information, please join our SAP Field Service Management Community page and explore our SAP Field Service Management Website. You can also watch the SAP Field Service Management 2502 Release Demo here, and I encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.

entry.title: Do you know how to manage your transportation processes effectively?
entry.content:

Overview

Learning objectives 
You will gain an overview of the key business transactions and system capabilities to enable you to design the processes you will use within your own organization. You will learn how SAP TM can help you to support Order Management, Transportation Planning, Subcontracting, Execution, Charge Calculation and Settlement.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – SAP Customer Engagement Initiative 2025-1 for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a complete, modular Cloud ERP software designed for every business need – powered by AI and analytics. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can run your mission-critical operations in real time from anywhere, introduce new business models in your industry, and expand globally with a trusted partner.  

An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has begun with the first cycle in 2025. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner, to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions. 

The projects will be open for customer registration until  March 28, 2025. 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud projects for registration: 

  • Bank Relationship Management: Improving Overview, Services & Fee, and Interest Calculations
  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • Joule and GenAI use cases in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies across different roles in a project
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape
  • Usability Improvements for managing attachments in S/4HANA business applications

Seize this opportunity to make your voices heard – register on influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025! If you are also interested in other SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects that are currently open for registration, check out the full list here. 

Warm regards, 

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Customizing of content form template in Output Management
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

To know more about this offering, please refer the blog:

Service with Advanced Execution

This blog showcases how the content form template can be customized to change the standard PDF generated by output control in service quotation.

Resource-Related Quotation

The resource-related quotation process begins when an execution order item is added in the service quotation. Once the execution order item is sent to planning by the service manager, it triggers the generation of a maintenance order. The maintenance order contains various operations and components based on planning, which are integral to fulfil the service requirements.

Once the planning is completed by maintenance planner, the planned data on the maintenance order is transferred to the service quotation via the DIP engine in the form of the resource-related cost line items. This are created as the subitems of main execution order item. These lines are distinguished from any other sub-items using the item category usage ‘RRCO’ (Resource-Related Cost).

To understand the process flow in Resource-Related Quotation, please refer the blog:

Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 3 – Planning and Cost Estimation in Service Quotation

Send to Customer

When the service quotation is sent to customer, the execution order item status is updated to ‘Released’. A standard PDF is generated by the output management which shows the details of the service quotation including items. This can be viewed in the Output Control assignment block. However, the resource-related cost items are not displayed in the standard PDF.

What is FDP?

FDP (Form Data Provider) is a gateway model that supplies the necessary data for generating output documents, such as invoices or purchase orders. It acts as the data source for a form template used in the output process, defining the structure and content of the document in the desired format.

What is output form?

Output form is a form which is used to display data in the PDF format to the customer where customer can see the items and subitems with respect to their pricing. 

Output form will read the data from FDP. 

Customizing Output Form 

The output PDF can be customized to enable display of resource-related cost line items as explained below.

Inside the form template, there is a provision to write scripts. The script must be modified to enable the resource-related cost line items in output form. 

The field SrvcQtnItmIsRsceReltdCostItm is already available in the node CRMS4S_SERVICEDOCUMENTITEMNODE for the OData FDP_CRMS4_SERVICE_QUOTATION.

This field value will be true if an item is resource-related cost item with item category usage as RRCO. This field will be consumed in the Script Section of the Adobe Print Form to suppress the RRCO line items in PDF output. The script will be edited using the Adobe Live Cycle Designer.

Step 1: Download the output form.

  • Go to t-code SFP (Form Builder).
  • Enter CRMS4_SERVICE_QTAN_SUMMARY in the form field.
  • Click on edit.
  • Click on Utilities -> Uploadingdownloading -> Downloading layout.
  • An XDP file will be downloaded.

Step 2: Modify the script in Adobe LiveCycle.

  • Open the XDP file in Adobe LiveCycle App.
  • In the Design view, the output format for the PDF can be seen.
  • In the rowTableSection -> rowTableItem, select the colItmCatUsg.
  • Open the Events with Scripts view in the Show option. The RRCO line items are suppressed in the output PDF by following logic. This code can be removed from the script to display the RRCO line items in output PDF.
  • Save the changes to the script.

Step 3: Upload the output form.

  • Go to t-code SFP (Form Builder).
  • Enter CRMS4_SERVICE_QTAN_SUMMARY in the form field.
  • Click on edit.
  • Click on Utilities -> Uploadingdownloading -> Uploading layout
  • Upload the modified XDP file.

After this step, when the service quotation is sent to the customer, the generated PDF will display resource-related cost items as well.

Summary

The standard PDF in the output control can be customized according to business requirement to enable or disable the display of resource-related cost items.

entry.title: Quick Tip: BRF+ trace in TM conditions troubleshooting
entry.content:
Photo by Andrea Piacquadio

Conditions are used in several different areas of TM, e.g. freight unit building rule determination, incompatibilities, change strategy determination etc. They are one of the features that makes TM so flexible. However complex conditions might prove challenging to troubleshoot. As TM conditions use BRF+ framework under the hood, BRF+ trace is an essential tool to diagnose them. 

BRF+ trace is activated with the parameter ID /SCMTMS/COND_EXPL = X in the SU3 transaction, the "Parameters" tab. That works beautifully, however there is a catch.

The note 1952185 - Performance improvement in condition processing: Buffering of BRFplus results has introduced TM conditions buffering that improves their performance. The note 1968935 - Activates the new Condition Buffer by standard has activated the buffer by default. 

Now, it can happen that a condition is correctly evaluated, but the BRF+ trace is not saved. That creates a false impression as if the condition has not be evaluated. To make sure that the trace is always saved, the buffering needs to be switched off. That can be done with another parameter ID /SCMTMS/COND_BUF = N.

Make sure to switch the buffering off only for testing, not to detriment production system performance.

The buffering side effect of BRF+ trace not being saved is not really documented explicitly. Thus, I am sharing this tip, so you do not stumble over that as I have recently.

entry.title: Unable to Read Repository at / Error Connecting to URL Error In Eclipse IDE-SAP BTP / S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Everyone , 

For the past few months / weeks I have been getting either of these two errors  

1. Error connecting to https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/ . 

2. unable to read repository  https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest or URL https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/<eclipse-version> . 

while installing the ADT Tools in the eclipse . 

I have extensively seen this error in the 2024-12 version of the IDE. 

there are many solutions being discussed around but I have found a rather simple solution , which I would like to share .

Steps : 

1. Go to https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/ for getting the ADT tools repository for your eclipse version .

2. select the repository  Link which you will be using to install the eclipse , in my case it will be https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/2024-12 .

3. Open the link in a new tab.

4. Save the Certificate for this site ; I'm using the Google Chrome browser , I will mention the steps for Microsoft Edge Below in this Blog .

  1. Click on the view site information icon (Left to the YRL) 

  2. Click on the Connection is Secure menu   

  3. Click on Certificate is Valid option 

  4. Go to the Details tab and click on Export button 

  5. Save the certificate with a name 

5. Apply the saved Certificate .

  1. Go to the saved Location and double click the certificate . 

  2. Click on Install Certificate  and choose Current User Click Next 

  3. Choose Automatically select the certificate store based on type of certificate option and choose next 

  4. Click Finish 

  5. You will get a popup saying import is done successfully  , Click Ok 

6. Now restart the eclipse and follow the steps to install ADT tools .

 

Steps to Save Certificate in Microsoft Edge Browser:

Once you are in the ADT tools link Page (Now you are in step 4)

  1. Click on the lock icon left to the URL 

  2. Click on the connection is secure menu 

  3. Click on the certificate button next to close button 

  4. Go to the Details tab and Click on Export button 

  5. Save your certificate 

  6. Now resume from step 5 

NB : This Blog is written solely from my experience , knowledge and my vision . If any misleading and wrong information is conveyed please correct me in the comments.

#Knowledge_sharing_is_caring
